wikidata_candidates.label: Claes Oldenburg
wikidata_candidates.reasoning: The records describe two different individuals. The index card identifies Howard H. Oldenburg, an engineer specializing in lighting systems and optical trains who worked for companies like Rambusch Lighting. The Wikidata entity (Q156731) identifies Claes Oldenburg, a famous pop art sculptor and artist. While they share the same surname and a geographic link to New York City, their names, occupations, and professional histories do not align.

results.possibleIdentity: Howard H. Oldenburg was a lighting engineer and technical specialist active during the late 1960s and 1970s. He is recognized for his collaboration with Albert Hamowy on technical art projects under the organization Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.). The duo, listed as 'Hamowy & Oldenburg,' participated in the landmark 1968 exhibition 'Some more beginnings' at the Brooklyn Museum, which featured works involving technical materials and processes. Professionally, Oldenburg was affiliated with Rambusch Lighting, a well-known lighting design firm based in New York City, and the American Electric Power Company (AEP). His technical expertise spanned lighting systems, optical trains, and various scientific instruments. Geographically, he was associated with the New York City metropolitan area and Flemington, New Jersey, where he served as the Mayor of Readington Township (Hunterdon County) in the early 1970s.
